hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 9, 6. You raised aggressively before the flop, putting pressure on your opponent, who calmly called your bet.
Flop — 2, Ace, 4. Your opponent checked, giving you the opportunity to bet, which they ultimately called.
Turn — 5. Both players checked, as the board failed to deliver any significant changes, leaving the situation unchanged.
River — 7. With the board still unchanged, your opponent decided to lead out with a bet, attempting to extract value from their hand.

wrongReasons: 10 7 plays similarly, but the correct hand pairs a specific board card that 10 7 doesn't. Look at which hole card ranks actually appear on the board.
10 3 makes a straight here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
With only high card, 10 6 wouldn't commit chips across multiple streets. This betting line requires a genuinely made hand from the flop onward.
hand.explanation:
Answer: A 6

Your opponent likely flopped a pair of Aces but played it cautiously, checking and calling on the flop, then checking on the turn, before finally deciding to bet on the river to extract thin value, indicating a medium-strength made hand of one pair.
